info.name: GEM Strategy API
info.description:
Global Equity Momentum signal API.

Based on Gary Antonacci's Dual Momentum Investing (2014). Computes 12-month (skip-1-month) momentum across SPY, ACWX, AGG, and BIL to determine the optimal ETF to hold each month.

---

**Setup:**
1. Make sure the server is running: `python server.py`
2. The `base_url` variable is set to `http://localhost:8000` — change it if you deploy elsewhere.
3. Run the requests in order — start with Health Check to confirm the server is up.

variable.description: Base URL of the GEM API server. Change this if running on a different host or port.

item.name: Meta
item.description: Service-level endpoints — health check and API info.

request.description:
Returns the service status and whether the daily signal cache is populated.

Use this to confirm the server is running and to check when the cache was last refreshed.

item.name: Signal
item.description: Current GEM signal endpoints — what to hold right now.

request.description:
Returns the current GEM signal — which ETF to hold right now.

Result is cached and recomputed once per calendar day. The `cached` field tells you whether this response was freshly computed or served from cache.

**Signal logic:**
1. RELATIVE momentum — SPY vs ACWX (12-month, skip-1-month)
2. ABSOLUTE momentum — equity winner vs BIL
   - Equity beats BIL → hold the equity winner
   - Equity below BIL → rotate to AGG (safe haven bonds)

response.name: 200 OK — hold US equities
200 OK — rotate to safe haven (AGG)
503 Service Unavailable — Yahoo Finance down

request.description:
Returns only the raw 12-month momentum scores for all 4 ETFs, without the full signal decision.

Useful for charting or comparing instruments directly. Served from the same daily cache as `/signal`.

response.name: 200 OK

exec: pm.test('Each score has price_then, price_now, momentum_pct', () => {

item.name: History
item.description: Historical GEM signal endpoints — monthly backfill with no look-ahead bias.

request.description:
Returns the GEM signal for each month-start over the past 24 months (default).

Each entry uses only data available at that rebalance date — no look-ahead bias. Most-recent month is listed first.

⚠️ This endpoint fetches fresh data on every call and may take a few seconds.

response.name: 200 OK

exec: pm.test('Each entry has all required fields', () => {
